title: Browser Trust, CORS, CSRF, Cookies, and Security Headers · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for browser trust, cors, csrf, cookies, and security headers and demands the smallest safe browser request and response-sharing policy matrix with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for browser request and response-sharing policy matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Implement an exact origin allowlist, preflight method and field checks, credentials, exposed fields, Vary, cache duration, null origin policy, and failure behavior.
Separate browser response-sharing controls from server authentication, authorization, network clients, and cross-origin request sending.
Protect cookie-authenticated mutations with safe-method semantics, tokens, trusted Origin or Referer, Fetch Metadata, and explicit exceptional-client handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Security Header Policy
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 5369 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to keep CORS response sharing distinct from CSRF mutation authorization; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then rotate a signing key while an old valid session remains within policy. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Deploy CSP, frame-ancestors, HSTS at the correct authority, MIME sniffing prevention, referrer policy, permissions policy, and cache controls with browser tests.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

For Node and Express server Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Security Header Policy, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Installing Helmet proves every browser security header is correct for every route.
